beginner.question: What does justify-items do in CSS Grid?
beginner.answer: justify-items aligns grid items along the inline (row) axis within their grid cells. Values: start, end, center, stretch.

beginner.question: What does align-items do in CSS Grid?
beginner.answer: align-items aligns grid items along the block (column) axis within their grid cells. Values: start, end, center, stretch.

beginner.question: What is the difference between justify-items and justify-content?
beginner.answer: justify-items aligns items inside their cells, while justify-content aligns the entire grid tracks within the container along the inline axis.

beginner.question: What does justify-self do on a grid item?
beginner.answer: justify-self overrides the container's justify-items for a single grid item, aligning that item along the inline axis.

beginner.question: What does align-self do on a grid item?
beginner.answer: align-self overrides the container's align-items for a single grid item, aligning that item along the block axis.

beginner.question: What is the default value of justify-items and align-items?
beginner.answer: The default value for both is stretch, meaning items fill their entire grid cell area by default.

beginner.question: What does align-content do in a grid container?
beginner.answer: align-content distributes space between and around grid tracks along the block (column) axis when there is extra space in the container.

beginner.question: What are the possible values for justify-content and align-content?
beginner.answer: start, end, center, stretch, space-between, space-around, space-evenly.

beginner.question: Can you use justify-self on a grid container?
beginner.answer: No, justify-self is applied to grid items, not the container. The container uses justify-items or justify-content.

beginner.question: What happens when a grid item has a smaller size than its cell?
beginner.answer: The item can be aligned within the cell using justify-self and align-self properties.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between space-between and space-evenly for justify-content?
intermediate.answer: space-between puts equal space between tracks but no space at the edges; space-evenly puts equal space everywhere including edges.

intermediate.question: How does align-content: center differ from align-items: center?
intermediate.answer: align-content: center centers the entire set of grid tracks in the container vertically; align-items: center centers items within their individual cells.

intermediate.question: What is the stretch value in justify-items and when does it not work?
intermediate.answer: stretch expands items to fill the cell. It does not work when the item has an intrinsic aspect ratio or an explicit width/height set.

intermediate.question: How do baseline values work in grid alignment?
intermediate.answer: align-items: baseline aligns the content baselines of items within a row, making text align across cells regardless of item padding.

intermediate.question: Can you use alignment properties with subgrid?
intermediate.answer: Yes, alignment properties work within subgrids. The subgrid's items align within their own cells based on the subgrid's alignment settings.

intermediate.question: What is the relationship between place-items and justify-items/align-items?
intermediate.answer: place-items is the shorthand for align-items and justify-items in that order: place-items: <align-items> <justify-items>.

intermediate.question: What is the place-content shorthand?
intermediate.answer: place-content is shorthand for align-content and justify-content: place-content: <align-content> <justify-content>.

intermediate.question: What is the place-self shorthand?
intermediate.answer: place-self is shorthand for align-self and justify-self on individual items: place-self: <align-self> <justify-self>.

intermediate.question: How does justify-content interact with fixed-width tracks?
intermediate.answer: If the total track width is less than the container, justify-content distributes the remaining space around or between tracks.

intermediate.question: What happens when align-content is used with grid-auto-flow: column?
intermediate.answer: align-content distributes space between rows in the block axis, while with grid-auto-flow: column items are placed in columns first.

advanced.question: How does the safe and unsafe alignment modifier work in Grid?
advanced.answer: safe ensures the item is not rendered outside the container to avoid data loss, while unsafe allows overflow if needed. Example: align-items: safe center.

advanced.question: How do alignment properties resolve when grid items have different aspect ratios?
advanced.answer: With stretch, items with intrinsic aspect ratios (like images) may not stretch in both axes. Use object-fit: cover on such items instead.

advanced.question: What is the interaction between align-content and overflow in a grid container?
advanced.answer: If content overflows the container, align-content may have no visible effect because there is no extra space to distribute.

advanced.question: How does grid item alignment interact with writing modes?
advanced.answer: Align and justify axes swap in vertical writing modes: justify-items aligns in the inline axis (vertical), align-items in the block axis (horizontal).

advanced.question: What happens to alignment when using grid-template-areas with empty cells?
advanced.answer: Empty cells still exist as grid areas, and alignment properties apply to the cells themselves. Items align within their assigned named areas.

advanced.question: How does justify-content: stretch work in a grid?
advanced.answer: stretch for justify-content increases track sizes to fill the container if minmax constraints allow it. If tracks have fixed sizes, stretch has no effect.

advanced.question: How do alignment properties interact with CSS Grid's span and gap simultaneously?
advanced.answer: Gap takes priority over alignment distribution. After gap is subtracted, remaining space is distributed by content/justify-content.

advanced.question: What is the difference between align-items: first baseline and align-items: last baseline?
advanced.answer: first baseline aligns using the baseline of the first line of text in each item; last baseline uses the last line of text in each item.

advanced.question: Can alignment properties override grid item margins?
advanced.answer: Yes, margins on grid items are factored into alignment calculations. Items with margin: auto can be centered independently of justify-self.

advanced.question: How does justify-items: legacy work in Grid?
advanced.answer: The legacy value inherits alignment behavior from the parent and is mainly for backwards compatibility with HTML align attributes; it behaves like start in Grid.
